title: helianthus × glaucus
intro: Helianthus × glaucus, also known as Sunflower-leaved False Sunflower, is a flowering plant species in the daisy family (Asteraceae). It is a perennial plant native to the prairies and open woods of eastern North America. Helianthus × glaucus has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of digestive issues, fever, and skin conditions. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, making it a valuable resource for natural health remedies.
